title: Sergeant Ted Grubbs Mental Healthcare for Disabled Veterans Act
short_title: Sergeant Ted Grubbs Mental Healthcare for Disabled Veterans Act
plain_summary: This bill requires the Department of Veterans Affairs to provide mental health care to certain disabled veterans within five days of when they ask for help. The bill only applies to veterans who have mental health problems caused by their military service that are rated as fifty percent disabled or higher. Currently, the VA has different rules for how quickly veterans can get care, but this bill creates a special fast rule just for these veterans with serious service-connected mental health conditions. The bill is named after Sergeant Ted Grubbs and changes the existing healthcare access rules to make sure these veterans get mental health treatment quickly.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Health.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Sergeant Ted Grubbs Mental Healthcare for Disabled Veterans Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Department of Veterans Affairs Community Care Access Standards for Certain Veterans with Mental Disorders
sections.text: This section changes the rules for how quickly the Department of Veterans Affairs must provide mental health care to certain veterans. If a veteran has a mental health condition that was caused by their military service and that condition is rated at fifty percent disabled or higher, the Department must provide hospital care, medical services, or long-term care services within five days of when the veteran asks for help. This requirement gets added to the existing access standards that the Department must follow.
